summary: Iter 11 — image upload endpoint + Today/Companion refactor regression. Backend image upload (POST /api/upload/image + GET /api/uploads/<file>) is solid: 7/7 pytest cases pass (PNG/JPEG accept, ext-rejection 400, >5MB-rejection 400, static-serve byte-exact, recipe POST persists /api/uploads/... URL). Today (/) regression PASS — all 5 extracted cards render (card-protected, card-daily-brief, streak-protector, card-life-progress, card-weekly-letter) with no console errors; the previous 'Compass is not defined' import error is gone. Companion (/companion) regression PASS — all 4 persona buttons + open-memories-btn + open-settings-btn render. Recipes dialog: add-recipe-btn, upload-recipe-image-btn, recipe-image-file-input, new-recipe-image, save-new-recipe-btn all present. URL paste → preview shows; clear-recipe-image-btn (X) clears it. CRITICAL FRONTEND BUG: selecting a file via the hidden input throws 'ReferenceError: handleFileUpload is not defined' — the onChange handler at /app/frontend/src/pages/Recipes.jsx:232 calls handleFileUpload(...) but no such function is defined anywhere in the file. This breaks the entire upload-from-file feature for this iteration.

ui_bugs.issue: ReferenceError: handleFileUpload is not defined. The file input's onChange={(e) => handleFileUpload(e.target.files?.[0])} calls a function that is never declared. Selecting a file crashes the page with the React dev-mode red overlay. No POST /api/upload/image is fired from the frontend. URL-paste flow still works because it bypasses this handler.

integration_issues.flow: Upload recipe image from file picker
integration_issues.issue: Frontend never calls POST /api/upload/image because handleFileUpload is missing. The backend endpoint is verified working via pytest (7/7 green) and would return {url, filename} correctly if invoked.

action_items: FIX (CRITICAL): Add the missing handleFileUpload function in /app/frontend/src/pages/Recipes.jsx. It should do roughly: (1) guard if !file, (2) setUploading(true), (3) build FormData with field name 'file', (4) call api.post('/upload/image', formData, {headers: {'Content-Type': 'multipart/form-data'}}), (5) setNewRecipe({...newRecipe, image: data.url}), (6) toast.error on failure, (7) setUploading(false) in finally, (8) clear fileRef.current.value so the same file can be re-picked. After the fix, re-test by re-running the file-upload Playwright flow — preview should appear and the URL input should remain empty (because image starts with /api/).
Once the function is added, verify the saved recipe card shows the uploaded image (background-image style with REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL prefix via the existing toAbsolute helper).
critical_code_review_comments: Recipes.jsx imports Upload and X icons and declares uploading/setUploading + fileRef but the actual handler function was never written — likely a copy/paste or merge miss. ESLint with no-undef rule would have caught this; recommend enabling no-undef in CRA's eslintConfig.

Recipes.jsx (300 lines) is borderline; if more recipe features land, consider extracting RecipeCreateDialog and RecipeDetailDialog into /components/recipes/* (mirroring the Today/Companion split done this iteration).

uploads.py allows .gif but does not validate magic bytes — relies on extension only. Acceptable for single-user trust model but worth noting; a malicious file renamed .png is accepted. Low severity here.
uploads.py reads full file into memory before the size check. For 5MB cap that's fine, but if cap ever rises, switch to chunked-read with running size accumulator and abort early.
server.py mounts StaticFiles at /api/uploads AFTER include_router(uploads) — order is correct (static catches GETs that the router doesn't define). No conflict observed.
toAbsolute() in Recipes.jsx correctly resolves /api/uploads/* against REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL — verified by inspection; once handleFileUpload is added, the e2e flow will be complete.

success_rate.frontend: ~75% — Today + Companion regressions clean; Recipes URL/clear flow works; file-upload flow broken by missing handleFileUpload function

seed_data_creation: TestRecipeWithUpload created+deleted a recipe titled 'TEST_iter11_recipe' and uploaded one PNG to /app/backend/uploads (file remains; cleanup of upload dir not implemented yet — may want a periodic sweep of orphaned uploads).
context_for_next_testing_agent: After main agent adds handleFileUpload to Recipes.jsx, re-run the Playwright file-upload flow: open Add recipe → set_input_files on [data-testid=recipe-image-file-input] → wait ~2s → assert [data-testid=new-recipe-image-preview] is visible AND input_value of [data-testid=new-recipe-image] is empty (because image starts with /api/). Then save recipe and verify the new card's background-image contains the absolute REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL + /api/uploads/... path. Backend endpoint is already proven via /app/backend/tests/test_iteration11.py (run with: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=$(grep REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL /app/frontend/.env | cut -d= -f2) python -m pytest backend/tests/test_iteration11.py -v).
rca_of_the_issue: ROOT CAUSE: /app/frontend/src/pages/Recipes.jsx line 232 references handleFileUpload(e.target.files?.[0]) inside the file input's onChange, but no such function is declared in the component. State (uploading/setUploading), the file ref (fileRef), and the icon imports (Upload, X) were all added, but the actual upload handler implementation was never written. REPRODUCTION: Visit /recipes → click Add recipe → click any file input or use page.set_input_files on [data-testid=recipe-image-file-input] → React shows red overlay 'ReferenceError: handleFileUpload is not defined'. NETWORK: zero POST requests are made to /api/upload/image. MITIGATION: define handleFileUpload as an async function inside the component scope (above saveRecipe) that builds FormData, POSTs to /upload/image via the existing api client, and updates newRecipe.image with response.data.url; toggle setUploading around the call and toast on failure.
